title: Chapter 2 — What the API Actually Exposes
CHAPTER 2 —
WHAT THE API ACTUALLY EXPOSES
segments.body_html:
The first thing to understand is that there is no single "Figma API." There are five overlapping programmatic surfaces,

Chapter 2 — What the API Actually Expose —

beats.text: The first thing to understand is that there is no single "Figma API." There are five overlapping programmatic surfaces, each with different permissions, plan requirements, rate limits, and read/write behavior. Most pipeline failures at the start of a project come from using the wrong surface , or from not knowing which surface gates the thing you need.

segments.label: 01 / 13 · HOOK

title: AUTHENTICATION AND
WHAT IT ACTUALLY CONTROLS
segments.body_html:
Every REST call requires an X-Figma-Token header. The value can be a Personal Access Token or an OAuth 2.0 token.

Authentication and What It Actually Cont — Personal Access Tokens are the simpler path. Generated in Figma account settings under Security, they carry exactly the

beats.text: Every REST call requires an X-Figma-Token header. The value can be a Personal Access Token or an OAuth 2.0 token. Personal Access Tokens are the simpler path. Generated in Figma account settings under Security, they carry exactly the permissions of the user who generated them. The token can access files the user can access. It cannot access files the user cannot. A token for a personal account does not reach into an organization's workspace unless that account has been explicitly added.

segments.label: 02 / 13 · AUTHENTICATION AND WHAT IT ACT

title: IN YOUR .ENV FILE —
NEVER COMMITTED
segments.body_html:
export FIGMA_TOKEN="figd_..."

In your .env file — never committed — Every CLI tool in this book reads FIGMA_TOKEN from the environment. That name is the stable convention. You will set it

beats.text: Every CLI tool in this book reads FIGMA_TOKEN from the environment. That name is the stable convention. You will set it once and every tool picks it up. The limitation worth knowing: PATs are per-user. A CI pipeline running with a PAT runs as that user. If the user loses access to the file , leaves the organization, has permissions revised , the pipeline breaks. For production pipelines, consider a dedicated Figma "bot" account.

segments.label: 03 / 13 · IN YOUR .ENV FILE — NEVER COMM

title: THE RATE
LIMIT ARCHITECTURE.
segments.body_html:
Rate limits are the most common reason a working script stops working at scale.

The Rate Limit Architecture — The documentation states that limits are applied per token, vary by endpoint, and vary by plan tier. A 429 Too Many Requ

beats.text: Rate limits are the most common reason a working script stops working at scale. The documentation states that limits are applied per token, vary by endpoint, and vary by plan tier. A 429 Too Many Requests response includes a Retry-After header indicating how many seconds to wait before retrying.

segments.label: 04 / 13 · THE RATE LIMIT ARCHITECTURE

title: THE ENTERPRISE
GATE.
segments.body_html:
·REST API read access to local variable collections
·REST API write access to variables
·Certain webhook event types may be plan-gated
·Full file graph read access
·Component and style metadata

beats.text: The Enterprise gate deserves its own treatment because it shapes the entire architecture of non-Enterprise pipeline design. This is not a complaint about pricing. It is a design constraint, and design constraints require design responses. What requires Enterprise, to the best of current knowledge :

segments.label: 05 / 13 · THE ENTERPRISE GATE

title: OPTIONAL, USED BY
WEBHOOK HANDLERS.
segments.body_html:
FIGMA_WEBHOOK_PASSCODE=your-passcode-here

Optional, used by webhook handlers — The file key comes from the Figma file URL. For a URL like the key is abc123XYZdef456.

beats.text: FIGMA_WEBHOOK_PASSCODE=your-passcode-here The file key comes from the Figma file URL. For a URL like the key is abc123XYZdef456.

segments.label: 06 / 13 · OPTIONAL, USED BY WEBHOOK HAND

title: .GITIGNORE
segments.body_html:
.env .env.local .env.*.local

.gitignore — In CI, inject FIGMA_TOKEN and FIGMA_FILE_KEY as environment secrets through your CI system's secret management. A 403 or

beats.text: In CI, inject FIGMA_TOKEN and FIGMA_FILE_KEY as environment secrets through your CI system's secret management. A 403 or 429 in CI almost always means the token or file key is misconfigured , not a code error.

segments.label: 07 / 13 · .GITIGNORE

title: FIGMA-PING.JS
segments.body_html:
·FIGMA_TOKEN and FIGMA_FILE_KEY are present in the environment
Auth validityGET /v1/me succeeds
File accessibility — a shallow GET /v1/files/key?depth=1 without pulling the full tree
Variables API access — GET /v1/files/key/variables/local and whether the response is a 403 (plan gate) or data
·Rate limit headroom from the response headers of the file ping

beats.text: figma-ping.js is a session health check. The premise is simple: before you write any extraction code, before you run any pipeline, you want to know definitively whether your authentication is valid, your file is accessible, your plan permits the endpoints you need, and your rate-limit headroom is acceptable. Discovering a stale token twenty minutes into a debugging session is expensive. Discovering it in ten seconds before you start is not.

segments.label: 08 / 13 · FIGMA-PING.JS

segments.section: WHAT THE PING CANNOT TELL YOU
title: WHAT THE PING CANNOT
TELL YOU.
segments.body_html:
The ping is a health check at a moment in time. It has failure modes worth knowing.

What the Ping Cannot Tell You — A shallow file fetch with ?depth=1 succeeds even on files that are too large to fully fetch without timing out. The ping

beats.text: The ping is a health check at a moment in time. It has failure modes worth knowing. A shallow file fetch with ?depth=1 succeeds even on files that are too large to fully fetch without timing out. The ping does not simulate a real pipeline load , it checks access, not capacity. A very large file may still fail on a full GET /v1/files/:key even after a passing ping.

segments.label: 09 / 13 · WHAT THE PING CANNOT TELL YOU

title: THE DOCUMENT
GRAPH MODEL.
segments.body_html:
When Figma launched their API in 2019 , it exposed the file graph for reading: document structure, component metadata, s

The Document Graph Model — The document graph model was borrowed from an older tradition: the DOM. Web browsers had long represented HTML documents

beats.text: When Figma launched their API in 2019 , it exposed the file graph for reading: document structure, component metadata, styles. This was a departure from the design tool APIs that preceded it, which were primarily export-oriented , give me a PNG of this artboard. The document graph model was borrowed from an older tradition: the DOM. Web browsers had long represented HTML documents as traversable trees where any node could be inspected programmatically. Figma applied the same model to design files.

segments.label: 10 / 13 · THE DOCUMENT GRAPH MODEL
